name: pydub (Simple Audio Manipulation)

auth.notes: Local library; ffmpeg required as external dependency

performance.notes: ffmpeg subprocess; slower than librosa for large files; good for format conversion

agent_readiness.notes: Simple audio file manipulation via ffmpeg. from pydub import AudioSegment. sound = AudioSegment.from_file('input.mp3'). sound[:5000] — first 5 seconds. sound + 6 — increase volume 6dB. sound1 + sound2 — concatenate. sound.split_on_silence(min_silence_len=500, silence_thresh=-40). sound.export('out.wav', format='wav'). Supports all ffmpeg formats. Overlay: combined = sound1.overlay(sound2, position=5000). Generators: Sine, WhiteNoise. Loudness normalization. Effects: fade_in, fade_out, speedup. Used for TTS preprocessing, podcast editing automation.

gotchas: Requires ffmpeg system dependency (not pure Python)
All audio loaded into memory — large files require chunking
Not suitable for real-time audio processing (use sounddevice/pyaudio)
best_when: Audio format conversion (MP3 to WAV for ML training data)
Silence detection and audio splitting for transcription pipelines
Simple audio concatenation and volume adjustment
avoid_when: Real-time audio I/O (use sounddevice or pyaudio)
Audio feature extraction for ML (use librosa)
Effects processing at quality (use pedalboard by Spotify)
summary: pydub provides simple audio manipulation via ffmpeg. AudioSegment.from_file('input.mp3'). sound[:5000] slices first 5s. sound + 6 adjusts volume by 6dB. Concatenate: a + b. split_on_silence() for voice activity detection. export() for format conversion. Supports all ffmpeg formats. Memory-based (load entire file). Requires ffmpeg. Best for audio preprocessing pipelines (silence splitting, format conversion). For real-time audio use sounddevice; for feature extraction use librosa.
